Mau Maus.description: A Puerto Rican street gang based in Brooklyn, New York, active from the late 1950s to the mid-1960s. Formed in 1957 by youths from the Fort Greene Housing Project, initially as the Mau Mau Chaplains. Known for its violent conflicts and the leadership of Nicky Cruz, who later became a Christian evangelist. The gang engaged in drug trafficking, murder, and other criminal activities.

descriptions: The Puerto Rican Mob lists the Mau Maus as a former rival.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Mau Maus (defunct)

descriptions: Mau Maus is listed as a rival of the Puerto Rican Mob.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: ... Mau Maus (defunct)

descriptions: The Mau Maus were formed by members who broke away from the Apaches.
edges.evidence_quote: Some members of a street gang called the Apaches broke away and created the Mau Maus

descriptions: The Mau Maus killed a member of the Bishops in retaliation for a killing.
edges.evidence_quote: Around January 1958 a member of the rival Bishop gang and a candy store owner were stabbed to death by some of the Mau Mau gang

descriptions: The Mau Maus initially merged with the Chaplains, forming the Mau Mau Chaplains.
edges.evidence_quote: They also asked permission from a rival gang called the Chaplains to start a Puerto Rican gang in the area. Eventually the gang was called Mau Mau Chaplains.

descriptions: The Mau Maus killed a member of the Sand Street Angels.
edges.evidence_quote: shot and killed Anthony Lavonchino, a member of the Sand Street Angels, one of the Mau Maus' enemies

descriptions: A former Mau Maus member founded and led the Vampires.
edges.evidence_quote: Salvador Agrón, a member of the Mau Maus, later led another gang called the Vampires

descriptions: The Mau Maus were bitter enemies of South Brooklyn Boys.
edges.evidence_quote: Also bitter enemies of SBB were the Puerto Rican Gangs, The Untouchable Bishops, and the Apaches, and the African American gangs The Mau Maus, and the Chaplains.

Benson Syndicate.description: A 19th-century criminal organization in the western United States led by John A. Benson. It fraudulently obtained and executed cadastral land survey contracts from the U.S. General Land Office, fabricating surveys and maps. Based in San Francisco, it was most active in California from the mid-1870s until 1898, infiltrating government positions to sustain its scheme.

edges.source: The Covenant, the Sword, and the Arm of the Lord

LRGP.description: A street gang based in the Broadway-Fillmore area of Buffalo, New York, founded in the 2000s. It was primarily involved in drug trafficking (marijuana and crack cocaine) and violent crimes, including murder, as part of a rivalry with the Bailey Boys. The gang was largely dismantled by federal and local law enforcement crackdowns between 2011 and 2015, and was formally declared defunct in 2016.

descriptions: Bailey Boys are rivals with LRGP.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: LRGP , Milla Gangsta Bloods

descriptions: LRGP is the direct opposition to the Bailey Boys, indicating a rivalry.
